query: hey so I need to add real-time chat to our app, it's a Next.js thing with Prisma and postgres. can you forge a spec for this? I want to think through the architecture before coding
what was I working on? I had some auth spec going I think, left off partway through phase 2
implement the spec — start from where I left off and work through the remaining tasks in phase 3
ok pause this spec, I need to context switch to something else. Save where I am so I can come back tomorrow
switch to the api-refactor spec, I want to work on that one instead. The dashboard spec can wait
write a spec for migrating our payment system from Stripe v2 to v3 API. We have about 15 webhook handlers and 3 checkout flows that need updating. I want a proper plan before touching anything
spec list — show me all my specs and their status. I think I have like 4 going right now
implement all phases of the current spec. just go through everything that's left
I want to plan out adding a notification system with SSE. Can you create a spec that covers the backend events, the SSE endpoint, and the frontend toast components?
generate openapi docs from my Express routes — I have about 40 endpoints across 6 route files in src/routes/
add a dark mode toggle to the settings page. The component is in src/components/Settings.tsx and we're using Tailwind with CSS variables for theming
there's a bug in the login flow — when a user enters the wrong password 3 times, the account should lock but it's not doing that. Can you fix it? The handler is in src/auth/login.ts
can you explain how the middleware chain works in our Express app? I see there are like 5 middleware functions in src/middleware/ and I'm not sure what order they run in
refactor the UserService class to use repository pattern instead of calling Prisma directly. All the database calls are in src/services/user.service.ts
write unit tests for the payment webhook handler in src/webhooks/stripe.ts — cover the checkout.session.completed, invoice.paid, and customer.subscription.deleted events
help me set up a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that runs tests on PR, builds the Docker image, and deploys to our staging k8s cluster. We're using pnpm and turbo
the /api/users endpoint is returning 500 when there are more than 1000 users. I think it's a pagination issue — can you look at src/routes/users.ts and fix the query?
what's the best approach for handling file uploads in our Next.js app? Should we use presigned S3 URLs or go through our API? The files are mostly images under 5MB
I need to update the Prisma schema to add a comments table with foreign keys to users and posts. Then generate the migration and update the types
review the changes in my last 3 commits — I refactored the auth module and want to make sure I didn't break anything. The tests pass but I want a second pair of eyes
